perturbation.original_text: 1.6 Certain Definitions . As used in this Agreement: 2 (a) “Affiliate” means, with respect to any Person, a Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, Controls, is Controlled by, or is under common Control with, such Person; provided that a Person will not be deemed to Control another Person if the only indicia of such Control is voting control of more than 10% but less than 20% of outstanding, publicly traded equity securities of such other Person. (b) “Control” (including the terms “Controlled by” and “under common Control with”) means, as used with respect to any Person, possession, directly or indirectly or as a trustee or executor, of power to direct or cause the direction of management or policies of such Person (whether through ownership of voting securities, as trustee or executor, by agreement or otherwise). (c) “Person” means an individual, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, joint venture, trust, unincorporated organization or other entity. (d) Other terms used but not defined herein have the respective meanings given to such terms in the Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 Certain Definitions . As used in this Agreement: (d) Other terms used but not defined herein have the respective meanings given to such terms in the Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the definitions of "Affiliate", "Control" and "Person" creates ambiguity and in-text contradiction. Several clauses, such as 1.3 (Non-Solicitation) refer to "Affiliates". Without a definition, the scope of the non-solicitation clause becomes unclear and thus unenforceable. This also generates contradictions in the definitions used in the Non-Solicitation clause, since it no longer can give a true reading of what it means for an Affiliate to solicit employees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 Non-Solicitation . During the Restrictive Period, without the prior written consent of the Buyer (which Buyer may withhold in the exercise of its sole, absolute, and arbitrary discretion), Sellers (including, without limitation, any successors) shall not induce or solicit, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any other person, (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise) any employee of the Buyer or its Affiliates who is involved in the managed modem business conducted by Buyer or any of its Affiliates, including, without limitation, the Business, to terminate the employee’s employment with the Buyer or any such Affiliate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the definitions of "Affiliate", "Control", and "Person", which are essential for interpreting clause 1.3 (Non-Solicitation). Clause 1.3 references 'Affiliates' and their employees. Without the definition of "Affiliate", the scope and enforceability of the non-solicitation clause are significantly undermined, creating a direct contradiction, because the absence of the definition creates ambiguity and prevents a clear understanding of who is covered by the non-solicitation agreement. This is a strong in-text contradiction as the modified text directly impacts the application of clause 1.3.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Applicable Law, Jurisdiction, and Venue . This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the Laws of the State of Colorado applicable to agreements made and to be performed wholly within such jurisdiction. The parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Colorado and of the United States of America, in each case located in the County of Denver, for any litigation arising out of or relating to this Agreement, and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to its respective address set forth in this Agreement shall be effective service of process for any litigation brought against it in any such court. The parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally waive any objection to the laying of venue of any litigation arising out of this Agreement in the courts of the State of Colorado or the United States of America, in each case, located in the County of Denver, and hereby further 3 irrevocably and unconditionally waive and agree not to plead or claim in any such court that any such litigation brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Applicable Law, Jurisdiction, and Venue . This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the Laws of the State of Colorado. The parties hereby consent to the jurisdiction of the courts for any litigation arising out of or relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrases 'applicable to agreements made and to be performed wholly within such jurisdiction,' 'exclusive jurisdiction,' the specific location of the courts (County of Denver), the method for service of process (U.S. registered mail), and the waiver of objection to venue, the clause becomes significantly weaker. It now only states Colorado law applies and that the parties consent to jurisdiction generally. The contract language loses its teeth on exactly the court that the case will occur. This weakens the original intention to guarantee venue in Denver, and now contradicts any assertion of guaranteed jurisdiction to that court.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Applicable Law, Jurisdiction, and Venue . This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the Laws of the State of Colorado. The parties hereby consent to the jurisdiction of the courts for any litigation arising out of or relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified exclusive jurisdiction in Denver County, a method for service of process (U.S. registered mail), and waiver of objections to venue. The changed text removes these specifics, only stating Colorado law applies and consenting to jurisdiction generally. This creates a contradiction because the original intent to guarantee venue in Denver is weakened, and the changed text no longer ensures that specific venue. This directly contradicts the expectation set by the original, more detailed clause, leading to potential ambiguity and disputes over jurisdiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Attorney Fees . If any action at law or in equity is brought to enforce or interpret the terms of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to its reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs, expert witness fees and all other disbursements in addition to any other relief to which it or he may be entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 Attorney Fees . If any action at law or in equity is brought to enforce or interpret the terms of this Agreement, the parties shall be responsible for their own fees.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'the prevailing party shall be entitled to its reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs, expert witness fees and all other disbursements' to 'the parties shall be responsible for their own fees', we directly contradict the previously stated term. This contradicts the intent that the prevailing party will be reimbursed and thus have a legal advantage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Attorney Fees . If any action at law or in equity is brought to enforce or interpret the terms of this Agreement, the parties shall be responsible for their own fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the original text, which stated the prevailing party would be entitled to attorneys' fees, with a statement that each party is responsible for their own fees. This directly contradicts the principle that the prevailing party can recover attorney fees, creating a clear conflict within the document. The contradicted text and changed text are the same statement, thus causing a contradiction when comparing the original text and changed text.

perturbation.original_text: 2.9 Entire Agreement . This Agreement and the Purchase Agreement and the documents referred to therein constitute the entire agreement among the parties pertaining to the subject matter hereof and supersede all prior agreements, letters of intent, understandings, negotiations and discussions of the parties, whether oral or written.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.9 Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement among the parties pertaining to the subject matter hereof and supersede all prior agreements, letters of intent, understandings, negotiations and discussions of the parties, whether oral or written.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and the Purchase Agreement and the documents referred to therein", the modified clause suggests that this agreement stands alone. This creates a contradiction in regards to Section 1.2, where it states: "Notwithstanding the foregoing, Sellers may, during the 60-day period following the Effective Date, provide services in the Business to those customers delineated on Schedule 1.02(c) of the Purchase Agreement, subject to and in accordance with Section 5.08 of the Purchase Agreement" This clause refers directly to the Purchase Agreement, which is now excluded from the Entire Agreement Clause. Therefore, this creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 Non-Competition . Sellers shall not engage in any business activity which is in competition with the Business conducted by Buyer. Because Sellers’ Business is nationwide, Sellers agree that they will not so compete anywhere in the United States (the “ Territory ”). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Sellers may, during the 60-day period following the Effective Date, provide services in the Business to those customers delineated on Schedule 1.02(c) of the Purchase Agreement, subject to and in accordance with Section 5.08 of the Purchase Agreement; provided such services shall be provided solely pursuant to the customer contracts listed on such Schedule 1.02(c) and the amount of such service shall not be materially greater than that provided to such customers by Sellers during the 60-day period prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes the Purchase Agreement as part of the entire agreement, while the changed text omits it. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 1.2, which explicitly references and relies on the Purchase Agreement and its schedules (Schedule 1.02(c) and Section 5.08) for defining the scope of permitted activities. By excluding the Purchase Agreement, the changed text suggests that the terms within that agreement, referenced by section 1.2, are not part of the overall agreement, therefore contradicting the reference to the Purchase Agreement mentioned in Section 1.2.
